data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix utilizes a microservices architecture to enable independent development, deployment, and scaling of different services.
data.technical_spec: Microservices communicate over REST APIs; use of Spring Boot, Hystrix, and Zuul for service management and routing.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Parts of Netflix's infrastructure run on serverless platforms to handle asynchronous workloads efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS Lambda for serverless execution to manage compute resources dynamically.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Netflix uses CDNs to cache and deliver content efficiently to a global audience.
data.technical_spec: Deployment on Open Connect enables localized streaming through a network of caching servers.

data.title: Data Pipeline
data.description: Netflix's data pipeline manages large volumes of data processing through real-time analytics and batch processing.
data.technical_spec: Apache Kafka and Flink are used for stream processing, while Parquet and Presto are used for data storage and querying.

data.title: Automated Deployment System
data.description: A continuous deployment system ensures rapid and reliable delivery of software updates.
data.technical_spec: Spinnaker is utilized for multi-cloud deployment management to improve release speed and reduce errors.
